sections.heading: What is Mindfulness?
sections.body: Mindfulness is paying attention to the present moment without judgment. It's not about clearing your mind — that's impossible. It's about noticing when your mind wanders and gently bringing it back. Think of it as a bicep curl for your attention: every time you notice your mind has drifted and return to the present, you're strengthening your focus muscle.

sections.heading: Why Developers Benefit from Mindfulness
sections.body: Programming requires sustained attention. Mindfulness trains the exact mental skills developers need: (1) Focus — staying on one task without context-switching. (2) Awareness — noticing bugs, edge cases, and architectural issues. (3) Patience — sitting with a difficult problem without giving up. (4) Resilience — bouncing back from frustrating code reviews or production incidents. Google, Apple, and Intel all offer mindfulness programs to their engineers for these reasons.

sections.heading: Your First Meditation (3 Minutes)
sections.body: You don't need an app, a cushion, or 30 minutes. Here's the simplest possible meditation:

code.snippet:
3-MINUTE MEDITATION:

1. Sit comfortably (chair is fine, no need for cross-legged)
2. Close your eyes or look at the floor
3. Focus on your breath — just notice the sensation
4. Your mind WILL wander (to work, lunch, that bug)
5. When it wanders, gently return to your breath
6. That's it — the 'wandering and returning' IS the practice

DON'T:
• Try to clear your mind (impossible)
• Judge yourself for getting distracted (everyone does)
• Think you're 'bad at meditation' (there's no bad)

DO:
• Start with 3 minutes, not 30
• Be consistent (daily > longer but sporadic)
• Notice the contrast before/after

TIP: Set a gentle alarm. When it rings, notice how you feel
compared to when you started. Most people feel calmer and
more focused — even after just 3 minutes.

jargon.definition: Paying attention to the present moment without judgment. A mental skill trained through practice, not a state you achieve.
A formal practice of training your attention. Like going to the gym for your mind — you're building focus and awareness.
The only moment that actually exists. Most stress comes from worrying about the future or ruminating about the past.
Observing thoughts and feelings without labeling them as 'good' or 'bad.' This reduces emotional reactivity.

summary: Mindfulness is paying attention to the present moment without judgment. It trains focus, awareness, patience, and resilience — skills developers need daily. Start with 3 minutes: sit comfortably, focus on your breath, and gently return when your mind wanders. The wandering and returning IS the practice.
